_description: Published Allan deviation values from chip-scale Rb-87 CPT atomic clocks. Each entry cites a specific paper figure or table. M3 calibration audit compares twin sigma_y(tau) against these reference numbers under matched-disturbance recipes.
_calibration_tolerance: factor of 2x (0.5 < ratio < 2.0) per the plan's defense in 'Public-data calibration tolerance — defense'. Published values vary by 1.5-3x between papers on similar hardware due to differences in cell setpoint, buffer-gas mix, beam diameter, and laser linewidth.

sources.citation: Kitching, J. (2018). Chip-scale atomic devices. Applied Physics Reviews, 5(3), 031302.

operating_conditions.notes: Review paper; values are representative of the Knappe-NIST CSAC architecture. Allan deviation curve shown in Figure 3 of the review, reproducing the canonical CSAC numbers.

sources.citation: Knappe, S., Shah, V., Schwindt, P. D. D., Hollberg, L., Kitching, J., Liew, L.-A., Moreland, J. (2004). A microfabricated atomic clock. Applied Physics Letters, 85(9), 1460-1462.
sources._note_doi_correction: The 2004 paper is in APL not Optics Letters per Kitching review citation; either way, this is the foundational chip-scale CPT result.

operating_conditions.notes: First chip-scale CPT clock demonstration. Paper reports Cs-133 not Rb-87, but the lock loop architecture and Allan deviation scaling are the canonical reference for the CSAC paradigm. We CAN compare our Rb-87 twin against this paper's curve shape but not absolute number unless we account for the Cs-vs-Rb hyperfine ratio (9192 vs 6835 MHz — only ~30% difference in fractional frequency).

sources.citation: Knappe, S., Schwindt, P. D. D., Gerginov, V., Shah, V., Liew, L., Moreland, J., Robinson, H. G., Hollberg, L., Kitching, J. (2005). Atomic vapor cells for chip-scale atomic clocks with improved long-term frequency stability. Optics Letters, 30(18), 2351-2353.

operating_conditions.notes: Long-term stability characterization paper. Drift coefficient of 5e-9/day reported.

allan_points.citation_note: Long-term floor where drift kicks in

sources.citation: Microsemi Corporation. (2014). SA.45s Chip Scale Atomic Clock (CSAC) datasheet rev. F.
sources._note: Now sold under Microchip part number SA65 / CSAC-SA65.

operating_conditions.notes: Commercial reference. Datasheet specs are conservative.

sources.citation: Liu, et al. (2025). Microcell atomic clock using laser current-actuated power modulation with 10^-12 range stability at 1 day. Scientific Reports.

operating_conditions.notes: Recent (2025) microcell SOTA. Uses laser-current power modulation rather than direct microwave injection.

calibration_strategy.scoring_rule: For each (source, tau): compute ratio = twin_sigma_y / source_sigma_y. PASS if 0.5 < ratio < 2.0 for the primary target on tau in {1, 10, 100, 1000}. Cross-checks (other sources) are reported but do not gate.
calibration_strategy.rb_vs_cs_caveat: Most published CSAC numbers are Cs-133; our twin is Rb-87. The hyperfine frequencies differ (9192 MHz Cs vs 6835 MHz Rb), and the natural-linewidth and buffer-gas dynamics differ slightly. We expect Rb-87 to land within a factor of 1.5 of comparable Cs-133 results — within the 2x tolerance.

matched_disturbance_recipes.kitching_2018_csac: clean (constant T=360 K, B=50 uT, I=1.0)
matched_disturbance_recipes.knappe_2005_apl_long_term: thermal_ramp (slow T drift to expose the buffer-gas-shift mechanism that the paper isolates)
matched_disturbance_recipes.microsemi_sa45s_datasheet: clean (datasheet specs are short-term stability under benign conditions)
